demographics.living_situation: lives independently; about to depart for a three-month remote occupational deployment

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I need my contraceptive injection tonight — I'm an established Depo user and I'm due for my next shot. I leave for the mines at 4 AM for three months and my period just started yesterday, so I want to get it in before I go.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Established user requesting her next scheduled long-acting contraceptive injection before leaving for a remote deployment where she will not have medical access; couldn't get an in-hours appointment.

symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: she is an established user due for her routine 12-weekly injection
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she says she currently has no periods most of the time with some light spotting, which she was told to expect on this method
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: none — she feels well and is fully functional; her only concern is timing the injection before she leaves

past_medical_history.how_managed: well, no regular doctor visits needed

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works away at a remote mining site on a three-month rotation starting at 4 AM; wants continuous contraceptive cover while deployed; reports she is happy with her current method and denies any low mood or unacceptable weight change

objective_data_offered.value: she says the skin over her left upper arm looks normal and healthy — no redness, broken skin or lumps
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-inspection, not a clinician examination of the injection site
